summary: Reis argues that data architecture is entering a 'Postmodern' era characterized by AI agents, probabilistic systems, and action-oriented workflows that move beyond traditional deterministic, dashboard-centric approaches. The shift is from analytics that tell you what happened to autonomous systems that take action, negotiate with suppliers, and optimize entire workflows without human intervention.
key_points: AI is moving from UI wrappers to core system architecture
The real value of AI is answering 'how' and 'why' not just 'what' and 'when'
We're shifting from dashboards to decision engines, from analysts to autonomous agents
Vibe coding introduces post-truth architecture where idempotency is no longer guaranteed
The Postmodern Data Stack embraces contradiction, fragmentation, and ambiguity
money_quotes: The real value of AI isn't in answering 'what' or 'when.' It's unlocking 'how' and 'why.'
We're moving from dashboards to decision engines. From analysts to autonomous agents. From 'let me know' to 'just do it or fix it.'
The old data stack was built for hindsight. This one is built for foresight, action, and even autonomy.
I half-joke that the apocalypse will happen because rogue AI agents perform DROP TABLE and remove all backups in every corporation and government system around the world.
Like its philosophical namesake, the Postmodern Data Stack embraces contradiction, fragmentation, and a deliberate ambiguity embodied in data architecture.
key_insight: The next evolution in data infrastructure isn't better dashboards or faster queries—it's autonomous agents that move from insight to action without human intervention, fundamentally changing the role of data teams from interpreters to architects of automated decision systems.
